title: fix: use /etc/containers/certs.d as default option
body:
#### What this PR does / why we need it:

See #10116

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #10116

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jonyhy96* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **jwhonce** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @jwhonce` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

That is fixing a problem that does not exist. See my other comment: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10116#issuecomment-979019630

I should have elaborated more.  The certs dir _is used_ but the defaults are set in containers/image, so Podman does not need to set anything.

I suggest to close this PR.

Why not change this PR to update the man pages so the man pages list this directory as default.
